data.title: Git-snapshot change-tracker with per-stage/per-phase bracketing and a claimed-vs-actual cross-check gate
data.executiveSummary: Replace the advisory flat `filesModified[]` with a structured `{filesCreated,filesModified,filesDeleted}` set and a new `src/tracking.ts` ChangeTracker that brackets EVERY stage (start+end) and EVERY phase (start+end) with a git snapshot, persisting an append-only `<specDir>/change-tracker.jsonl`. A git cross-check gate is AND-ed into phase-green (mirroring spec-10's deliverable AND): a phase that claims to create/modify a file git does not show changed hard-fails, closing the false-green root cause a second way. Tracking and spec-10 deliverable assertions collapse into one enforcement path (claimed.filesCreated auto-populates requireFiles); the whole subsystem never throws and degrades when git is unavailable.

acceptanceCriteria.statement: New `src/tracking.ts` exports a `ChangeTracker` constructed with `(specDir, worktreePath)`. `begin(unit: "stage"|"phase", id)` snapshots `git -C <wt> rev-parse HEAD` + `git -C <wt> status --porcelain` as the baseline; `end(unit, id, claimed?: {filesCreated,filesModified,filesDeleted})` re-snapshots and computes the DELTA as `git -C <wt> diff --name-status <beginHead>` (committed) UNION `git -C <wt> status --porcelain` (uncommitted/untracked), classifying into `gitActual:{created(A/+untracked), modified(M), deleted(D)}`, then CROSS-CHECKS vs `claimed` into `{claimedNotChanged[], changedNotClaimed[]}` and appends ONE jsonl line to `<specDir>/change-tracker.jsonl`. Reuses the existing `spawnSync` + `dedupePreservingOrder` git-union patterns from `src/build-runner.ts` (`touchedFilePaths`); no new runtime deps.

ChangeTracker is NEVER-throws: every git op is try/caught; on git failure it records `{gitUnavailable:true}` and returns a non-blocking `ChangeRecord` (verdict continues), so infrastructure absence never aborts a run. Conservative parse: a claimed file counts as `claimedNotChanged` only when git clearly shows no change.

Unit tests (mocked git) cover: created/modified/deleted classification; `claimedNotChanged` vs `changedNotClaimed`; git-unavailable → record-and-continue + non-blocking; begin/end bracketing emits start+end records; append-only jsonl (multiple `end` events → multiple lines, nothing overwritten).

ALL stages are bracketed (`tracker.begin("stage", stageId)` / `tracker.end("stage", stageId, claimed?)`) and implementation phases are ADDITIONALLY bracketed (`tracker.begin("phase", phaseId)` before the attempt loop / `tracker.end("phase", phaseId, claimedFromImplementer)` after). Implemented via a minimal-touch seam — PREFERRED an `ctx.events` "stage"/"phase" subscription or a runner-level wrapper (events already emitted in `src/nodes.ts` lines 98/126/127) over editing nodes.ts/workflow.ts/pipeline.ts internals. A synthetic-pipeline test asserts change-tracker.jsonl contains stage-start/stage-end for every stage AND phase-start/phase-end for the implementation stage, in correct nested order.

Tracker is threaded via ONE consistent mechanism mirroring `activeRun`/`setActiveRun`/`getActiveRun` in `src/extension.ts` (module-level per-run singleton set on execute() entry from `state.setup` providing `specDir`+`worktreePath`, cleared in the same finally). No leak between runs.

`buildImplementPrompt` (src/prompts.ts ~line 120) and the review/fix prompt (~line 140) change their output contract from `filesModified (array)` to `filesCreated (array), filesModified (array), filesDeleted (array)` with the instruction: "Report the exact files you created/modified/deleted — these are git-cross-checked; claiming a file you didn't change fails the phase." implementation.ts (~line 159) parses the structured set but STILL accepts a legacy flat `filesModified` array (backward-tolerant).

A `changeGate` is computed in `src/stages/implementation.ts` after `runBuildGate` (~line 165) and `runDeliverableCheck` (~line 185). `changeGate.pass === false` iff `claimedNotChanged` is non-empty (a created/modified claim git does NOT show) — the false-green killer; `changedNotClaimed` is advisory-only (logged, never fails). Phase GREEN (line 194) becomes `(gate.pass || gate.inScopePass) && deliverableCheck.pass && changeGate.pass`. A `claimedNotChanged` miss is fed into the next implementer attempt under a `## Claimed changes not present in git — actually create/wire these` section via the existing attemptErrors/missingDeliverables retry channel, respecting `MAX_ATTEMPTS`. Never throws; git-unavailable → `changeGate.pass = true`.

Regression test (mirroring `tests/implementation.test.ts` / `build-runner-deliverable-check.test.ts` patterns): a phase returning `claimed:{filesCreated:[X]}` where git shows NO new file → `changeGate.pass === false` → phase is NOT green, even though the build gate and deliverable check pass. The stockfan Phase-5/6 false-green (claimed a file that was never created/wired) becomes impossible.

Spec-10 bridge: before `runDeliverableCheck` is called, `claimed.filesCreated` is UNIONed with any spec-declared `deliverables.requireFiles`, so a file a phase claims to have created MUST exist — tracking and deliverable assertions reinforce rather than diverge.

The phase end-record's `gitActual` is surfaced as evidence: a `📝 N files changed (C/M/D)` line in the implementation summary, and the flat `filesModified` accumulation (implementation.ts lines 92/159/219) is derived from the structured set so the existing summary writer (prompts.ts line 127) keeps working.

`npm run typecheck` (tsc --noEmit) is clean and `npm test` (vitest run) is ALL green — existing suite plus new tracker/cross-check/structured-changes tests — with NO regression to runRedCheck, runDeliverableCheck, npm in-scope classification, scope-aware cargo gate, themed stream, mid-run input injection, dashboard, or real-theme parity (`tests/stream-theme-class-theme.test.ts`, `tests/render/real-theme-parity.test.ts` stay green). pi Theme methods are called method-style, NEVER destructured.

nonFunctional: Never-throw invariant: the tracker, all git ops, the cross-check, and the changeGate must degrade gracefully — git unavailable → record `{gitUnavailable:true}` and continue; parse ambiguity → conservative (treat claimed as not-changed only when git clearly shows no change); the subsystem sits in the hot path of EVERY run and must never abort or widen a phase's GREEN.

Performance: git snapshots are bounded `spawnSync` calls (`rev-parse`, `status --porcelain`, `diff --name-status`) under the existing `resolveTimeoutMs` envelope; one append-only jsonl record per begin/end event (no rewrites, no cross-phase caching that could go stale); N stages × M phases yields bounded, linear I/O. No new runtime dependencies.

Backward compatibility: legacy flat `filesModified` arrays from older agents are accepted; non-git worktrees are recorded as `gitUnavailable` and unblock (`changeGate.pass = true`); phases with no claimed changes trivially pass; no changes to backend selection (subprocess/session) or control-flow (branch/parallel/loop) semantics.

Security/hygiene: the tracker writes only into `specDir`; git is spawned with discrete argv (`-C <wt> ...`, consistent with build-runner.ts Gap-4 hygiene) so file paths from agents cannot inject shell; the cross-check is one-directional (the AGENT claims, the framework verifies) — the framework never auto-generates claimed changes from git.
openQuestions: Threading mechanism: confirm the module-level singleton mirroring `activeRun` (preferred, minimal touch) over a RunOptions/StageContext field — and verify behavior if two super-dev runs ever overlap in one process (today runs are serialized, but confirm the singleton is cleared in the execute() finally).

Record granularity: should `begin` events be persisted as `{event:"start"}` lines too (complete bracket trace) or only `end` records (smaller file)? The spec recommends start+end; confirm acceptable jsonl size for very long multi-phase runs.

Cross-check scope vs spec-declared deliverables: the spec says claimed-changes is one-directional enforcement — confirm that UNIONing `claimed.filesCreated` into `requireFiles` does not create a circular double-count (an agent that omits a required file the spec already declared is still caught by the spec-declared requireFiles).

Dashboard surfacing: is the `📝 N files changed (C/M/D)` line sufficient in the implementation summary, or does the dashboard also need a dedicated change-tracker panel consuming change-tracker.jsonl? (Listed as a future surfacing concern; out of scope for this cut unless confirmed.)

features.name: ChangeTracker Module — baseline, delta, cross-check, durable file

scenarios.title: Baseline captured when a stage or phase begins
scenarios.given: A change tracker initialized for a worktree under version control
scenarios.when: tracking begins for a unit (a stage or a phase)
scenarios.then: the current git revision and the working-tree state are captured as the baseline for that unit
andClauses: And that baseline is held pending until the unit ends

scenarios.title: Actual changes are computed and classified at unit end
scenarios.given: A unit whose baseline was captured at its start
scenarios.when: the unit ends along with the set of file changes the agent claimed
scenarios.then: the set of files actually changed since the baseline is computed from git
andClauses: And each changed file is classified as created, modified, or deleted
And committed changes and uncommitted or untracked changes are both included

scenarios.title: Cross-check separates vacuous claims from unreported edits
scenarios.given: A unit ends with a structured set of claimed file changes
scenarios.when: the claimed set is cross-checked against what git shows
scenarios.then: files the unit claimed to change but that git shows unchanged are flagged as claimed-but-not-changed
andClauses: And files git shows changed that the unit did not report are flagged as changed-but-not-claimed

scenarios.title: Every event is appended to a single durable tracking file
scenarios.given: A run that has produced several begin and end events
scenarios.then: one line is appended per event to a single durable file located in the spec directory
andClauses: And no previously written line is ever overwritten or lost
And each end record carries the actual changes, the claimed changes, the cross-check flags, and a verdict

features.name: Never-Throw Resilience — git unavailable and ambiguous parses

scenarios.title: Git unavailability is recorded without aborting the run
scenarios.given: A worktree where git is not available or a git command fails
scenarios.then: the tracker records that git is unavailable
andClauses: And the run continues without throwing
And the phase is not blocked by the infrastructure failure

scenarios.title: Ambiguous parse resolved conservatively
scenarios.given: A claimed file whose git status cannot be clearly resolved
scenarios.then: the claim is treated as not-changed only when git clearly shows no change
andClauses: And no false failure is introduced by parsing ambiguity

scenarios.title: Begin and end events bracket a unit completely
scenarios.given: A unit that has a captured baseline
scenarios.then: a start record and an end record are both present in the tracking file
andClauses: And multiple end events across a run each append their own line without collision

features.name: Stage and Phase Bracketing — every stage, nested phases

scenarios.title: Every stage produces a start and an end record
scenarios.given: A pipeline composed of several stages
scenarios.then: each stage contributes a stage-start record followed by a stage-end record in the tracking file

scenarios.title: Implementation phases are nested inside their stage records in order
scenarios.given: An implementation stage that executes several phases
scenarios.then: phase-start and phase-end records appear nested between the stage-start and stage-end records in the correct order
andClauses: And each phase bracket wraps its attempt loop

scenarios.title: Tracker state is scoped to a single run
scenarios.given: A tracker established as a per-run singleton at run entry using the spec directory and worktree path
scenarios.then: the tracker is cleared
andClauses: And no tracking state leaks into a subsequent run

scenarios.title: Code-mutating agents report created, modified, and deleted files
scenarios.given: An implementer or review/fix agent that changes files
scenarios.then: it reports separate sets of created, modified, and deleted files
andClauses: And the report is flagged as subject to git cross-check
And claiming a file the agent did not change is stated to fail the phase

scenarios.title: Legacy flat change list is still accepted
scenarios.given: An agent that returns only a legacy flat list of modified files
scenarios.then: the legacy list is accepted and normalized without error
andClauses: And the run does not regress for agents that have not adopted the structured contract

scenarios.title: Phase stays not-green when a claimed file was never changed
scenarios.given: A phase whose agent claimed to create or modify a file that was never created or wired
scenarios.when: git shows that file was not changed
scenarios.then: the change gate fails
andClauses: And the phase is not green even when the build gate and deliverable check both pass
And the historical false-green where a phase claims a file that never exists becomes impossible

scenarios.title: Unreported edits never fail a phase
scenarios.given: A phase where git shows edits the agent did not report
scenarios.then: the unreported edits are logged as advisory only
andClauses: And the change gate is not failed by under-reporting

scenarios.title: Missing claimed changes trigger a targeted retry within budget
scenarios.given: A phase that failed the change gate because of claimed-but-not-changed files
scenarios.then: the missing files are surfaced to the next attempt as changes that must actually be created or wired
andClauses: And the retry respects the maximum attempt count

scenarios.title: Phase with no claimed changes passes the gate trivially
scenarios.given: A phase that claims no created or modified files
scenarios.then: the gate passes without blocking

scenarios.title: Gate does not block when git infrastructure is unavailable
scenarios.given: A phase evaluated where git is unavailable
scenarios.then: the gate passes so the phase is not blocked by infrastructure failure
andClauses: And the gate never throws

features.name: Deliverable Assertion Bridge — tracking reinforces spec-10

scenarios.title: Claimed created files are enforced as required deliverables
scenarios.given: A phase that claims to have created a set of files
scenarios.then: the claimed created files are unioned with any spec-declared required files
andClauses: And each claimed created file must exist or the deliverable check fails

scenarios.title: Actual changes are surfaced as concise run evidence
scenarios.given: A phase whose end record captured the real git changes
scenarios.then: a concise count of created, modified, and deleted files is surfaced as evidence
andClauses: And the existing summary consumers continue to work from the structured set

features.name: Quality and Non-Regression — typecheck, tests, theme parity

scenarios.title: Typecheck and the full suite remain green with no regressions
scenarios.given: The repository after the change tracker is integrated
scenarios.when: the typecheck and the full test suite run
scenarios.then: the typecheck is clean and every test passes
andClauses: And the red check, deliverable check, npm in-scope classification, themed stream, mid-run input, dashboard, and theme-parity behaviors do not regress
And the pi Theme methods are called method-style and never destructured

traceability.description: ChangeTracker: begin snapshots baseline; end computes git delta (committed UNION uncommitted), classifies created/modified/deleted, cross-checks vs claimed into claimedNotChanged/changedNotClaimed, appends one jsonl line; reuses spawnSync + dedupePreservingOrder git-union patterns.

Never-throws: every git op try/caught; git failure records {gitUnavailable:true} and returns a non-blocking record; conservative parse treats a claim as not-changed only when git clearly shows no change.
Unit tests (mocked git) cover classification, claimedNotChanged vs changedNotClaimed, git-unavailable record-and-continue, begin/end bracketing emits start+end records, append-only jsonl.
All stages bracketed (stage start+end); implementation phases additionally bracketed (phase start+end) via minimal-touch event/runner seam; nested order asserted by a synthetic-pipeline test.
Tracker threaded via one consistent module-level per-run singleton mirroring activeRun, set at execute() entry from state.setup, cleared in finally; no leak between runs.

buildImplementPrompt + review/fix prompt change output contract to filesCreated/filesModified/filesDeleted with git-cross-check instruction; implementation.ts parses the structured set but tolerates legacy flat filesModified.

changeGate computed after build+deliverable gates: pass===false iff claimedNotChanged non-empty; changedNotClaimed advisory-only; failures fed into retry via existing channel respecting MAX_ATTEMPTS; git-unavailable -> pass=true; never throws.
Regression test: phase claiming filesCreated:[X] where git shows no new file -> changeGate.pass===false -> phase not green even if build and deliverable pass; false-green impossible.
Before runDeliverableCheck, claimed.filesCreated is unioned with spec-declared deliverables.requireFiles so a claimed-created file must exist.
Phase end-record gitActual surfaced as a 'N files changed (C/M/D)' summary line; flat filesModified accumulation derived from the structured set so existing summary writer keeps working.

npm run typecheck clean; npm test all green (existing + new); no regression to runRedCheck, runDeliverableCheck, npm in-scope, scope-aware cargo gate, themed stream, mid-run input, dashboard, real-theme parity; pi Theme called method-style, never destructured.

data.title: Git-Snapshot Change-Tracker & Cross-Check Gate: Research Brief
data.summary: The design is verified against the live codebase and every previously-open issue is resolved (see BP entries below). The four load-bearing seams all exist as the spec predicts: (1) `dedupePreservingOrder` at `src/build-runner.ts:118` is an unexported one-line-export fix (ISS-01 RESOLVED); (2) `touchedFilePaths` at `src/build-runner.ts:526` returns a FLAT `string[]` (committed `diff --name-only` UNION `ls-files --others`) and deliberately discards the git status letter, so it CANNOT classify created/modified/deleted — `tracking.ts` re-implements the two-bounded-spawns + dedupe-union PATTERN but with `diff --name-status <beginHead>` UNION `status --porcelain` to recover A/M/D (ISS-02 RESOLVED); (3) the cross-check is UNION-membership — a claimed file is `claimedNotChanged` only when absent from the full gitActual created∪modified set, not per-bucket (ISS-03 RESOLVED); (4) `changeGate` rides the existing `implParts` retry channel (`src/stages/implementation.ts:146-154`, the same channel `attemptErrors`/`missingDeliverables` use) via a decoupled `crossCheck()` that only adds a `## Claimed changes not present in git` section, with the stage seam being an `ctx.events` subscription and the phase seam being direct begin/end calls in implementation.ts threaded through the `activeRun`-mirror singleton (`src/extension.ts:68/131/148`, cleared in `finally` at :446) (ISS-04 RESOLVED). The phase-green AND-chain (`implementation.ts:194`) extends cleanly from `(gate.pass||gate.inScopePass) && deliverableCheck.pass` to `&& changeGate.pass`, mirroring spec-10's deliverable AND with asymmetric strictness: `claimedNotChanged` hard-fails (the false-green killer), `changedNotClaimed` is advisory-only (under-reporting ≠ false-green), and git-unavailability degrades to `changeGate.pass=true` (never blocks on infrastructure). The never-throw contract is established practice (`touchedFilePaths` try/catches to `[]`); the tracker records `{gitUnavailable:true}` and continues. No new runtime deps; the three git snapshots (rev-parse, status --porcelain, diff --name-status) reuse the existing discrete-argv `spawnSync` envelope with no `shell:true`. Sources: SRC-001 build-runner.ts:118; SRC-002 build-runner.ts:526; SRC-003 nodes.ts:95-98,126-127; SRC-004 extension.ts:68/131/148/446; SRC-005 implementation.ts:103-219 (159 parse, 165 build-gate, 184-194 green-AND, 153-154 implParts retry). Best practices: BP-001 never-throw git helpers (touchedFilePaths → [] on failure); BP-002 two bounded spawns + dedupe-union; BP-003 asymmetric cross-check (claim-vs-ground hard, ground-vs-claim advisory); BP-004 module singleton cleared in finally; BP-005 event-bus subscription over engine-internals edit.

options.name: A. Change-set extraction: new classified extractor (recommended) vs reuse touchedFilePaths
options.tradeoffs: Reuse `touchedFilePaths` (SRC-002) would be DRY but it returns flat string[] via `diff --name-only` + `ls-files --others` and INTENTIONALLY strips the A/M/D status letter, so it cannot feed the created/modified/deleted split or the cross-check. RECOMMENDED: a sibling `classifyChangedFiles(cwd, beginHead)` in tracking.ts using `diff --name-status <beginHead>` UNION `status --porcelain` (A/?? → created, M → modified, D → deleted), reusing the same two-bounded-spawns + `dedupePreservingOrder` (SRC-001, one-line export fix) never-throw envelope (BP-001/BP-002). Tradeoff: ~40 lines of near-duplicate git plumbing; justified because classification is the tracker's core deliverable and --name-status/--porcelain semantics (status letters, R rename detection, copy markers) differ from --name-only. Keep argv discrete (no shell:true).

options.name: B. Tracker threading: module-level singleton mirroring activeRun (recommended) vs RunOptions/StageContext vs ctx.events-only
options.tradeoffs: The activeRun/setActiveRun/getActiveRun triplet in extension.ts (SRC-004) is the proven per-run-singleton pattern — set on execute() entry from state.setup (already carries specDir+worktreePath), cleared in the same finally (:446). RECOMMENDED mirroring it: setActiveTracker/getActiveTracker set where setActiveRun is already called, cleared alongside it — zero inter-run leak, zero RunOptions signature churn, and the implementation.ts phase seam reads it without threading a new arg through every stage.run(state,ctx). Tradeoff vs ctx.events-only: events can capture STAGE enter/exit (SRC-003) but CANNOT carry the implementer's `claimed` set (lives in impl.control inside the phase loop), so a pure-event design still needs a direct call in implementation.ts — hence the hybrid: events for stages, direct calls for phases. RunOptions threading is the most explicit but touches the most call sites; overkill given the singleton already works for mid-run input.

options.name: C. Stage-boundary capture: ctx.events subscription (recommended) vs runner-level wrapper vs nodes.ts edit
options.tradeoffs: src/nodes.ts already emits `ctx.events.emit("stage", {id,label,status:"running"})` at :127 (enter) and `ctx.events.emit("stage", {id,label,status,error})` at :98 (exit, in the task-node closure, fired on both pass and fail). RECOMMENDED a thin tracker.onStageEvent subscriber wired once at execute() entry: status==="running" → begin("stage",id), else → end("stage",id) (claimed undefined for non-implementation stages — only gitActual evidence recorded). Fully decoupled from nodes.ts/workflow.ts/pipeline.ts internals (minimal-touch, satisfies AC-04's PREFERRED path, BP-005). Tradeoff: the event payload has no `claimed` field, so stage-level cross-check is unavailable — acceptable because the cross-check GATE is phase-scoped. A runner-level wrapper around stage.run would carry claimed but requires editing nodes.ts; reject unless events prove insufficient. Phase bracketing is NOT event-driven (the :126 phase emit is just a label marker) — two direct calls in implementation.ts: begin("phase",phaseId) before the for-attempt loop, end("phase",phaseId, claimed) after.

options.name: D. Cross-check strictness: asymmetric hard/advisory (recommended, spec-faithful) vs symmetric vs advisory-only
options.tradeoffs: RECOMMENDED the spec's asymmetric rule (BP-003): changeGate.pass===false iff claimedNotChanged non-empty (a created/modified file git shows unchanged = vacuous claim = the Phase-5/6 false-green root cause), while changedNotClaimed (git edits the agent didn't report) is LOGGED only. Rationale: claiming-done-but-undone is a correctness defect (false green); unreported edits are under-reporting (advisory, common with format-on-save). Per-bucket matching (ISS-03, RESOLVED) is deliberately NOT used — a file claimed `created` but git shows `modified` is NOT a failure (the change exists; classification fuzziness from rename/copy would cause false alarms); only claimed-and-totally-absent fails. Tradeoff vs symmetric hard-fail: a stricter gate risks flapping on R/C rename codes — mitigate by normalizing rename-targets (R99\told\tnew → include new) before the union. Tradeoff vs advisory-only: loses the false-green killer, the entire point of the feature. Feed claimedNotChanged into implParts under `## Claimed changes not present in git — actually create/wire these`, reusing the exact missingDeliverables→implParts retry shape (SRC-005, lines 153-154), respecting MAX_ATTEMPTS.

openIssues: All previously-open issues are RESOLVED and require no further research — documented here for traceability: ISS-01 (dedupePreservingOrder export): src/build-runner.ts:118 is an unexported `function` (grep-clean), confirmed one-line `export` addition; ISS-02 (touchedFilePaths cannot classify / tracking.ts re-implements the union): src/build-runner.ts:526 returns flat string[] via diff --name-only + ls-files --others, deliberately dropping the status letter, so tracking.ts needs its own diff --name-status + status --porcelain extractor reusing only the spawnSync+dedupe UNION pattern; ISS-03 (cross-check is UNION-membership not per-bucket): claimedNotChanged fires only when a claimed path is absent from the full gitActual created∪modified set (not created-vs-created / modified-vs-modified); ISS-04 (changeGate rides the existing implParts retry channel with a decoupled crossCheck()): the stage seam is a ctx.events "stage" subscription (nodes.ts:98 enter-via-running / 127 exit), the phase seam is direct begin/end calls in implementation.ts, and the tracker singleton is threaded via the activeRun-mirror pattern (extension.ts:68/131/148, cleared in finally :446). AC-04 stage seam confirmed viable via ctx.events. No new runtime deps; never-throw contract mirrors touchedFilePaths. No remaining open issues.

data.title: False-GREEN root cause: implementation phase-green has no assertion tying the agent's claimed file changes to git ground-truth
data.summary: On current `main` (worktree `11-git-change-tracker-crosscheck-gate`, branch ahead of nothing; only spec docs untracked; `src/tracking.ts` absent; no `change-tracker.jsonl` anywhere), an implementation phase goes GREEN on a VACUOUS file claim. The phase-GREEN decision in `src/stages/implementation.ts:194` is `(gate.pass || gate.inScopePass) && deliverableCheck.pass`. The implementer's claimed `filesModified` (parsed at `implementation.ts:159-160`, accumulated into the summary at `:92/:219`) is advisory-only and NEVER compared to git. `runDeliverableCheck` (`build-runner.ts:1789`) is spec-OPT-IN: when `phase.deliverables` is undefined it early-returns `{pass:true,missing:[]}` (SCENARIO-014 backward-compat), so a phase whose author declared no deliverables has NO assertion against its claimed file changes. The build gate passes trivially because a never-created/unreferenced file compiles clean (`runBuildGate` runs whatever exists). The conjunction of these three gaps is the false-green: a phase claims to create `src/tracking.ts` (or a stockfan `e2e_x.rs`), changes nothing in git, and still passes build + deliverables → GREEN. The summary then lies ("Files Modified: src/tracking.ts"). spec-10 closed this *partially* (opt-in deliverable assertions) but only for phases whose SPEC author declared deliverables; it left the AGENT's self-reported `filesModified` entirely unverified. This analysis confirms the "before" buggy state and defines the change-tracker cross-check gate (AC-01..AC-11) as the second, agent-claim-directional closure.
hypotheses: H1 (70%, CONFIRMED): The implementer's `filesModified` claim is advisory-only and never verified against git ground-truth. Prediction: a phase returning `filesModified:[X]` where git shows no change to X still goes GREEN. Evidence: `implementation.ts:159-160` only ACCUMULATES the array into `filesModified[]`; `:194` phase-GREEN never reads it; `prompts.ts:127` surfaces it in the summary as an unverifiable string. No `git diff`/`status` cross-check exists anywhere in the phase loop.

H2 (20%, CONFIRMED): The deliverable oracle passes trivially for undeclared deliverables, removing the only existing assertion layer. Prediction: a phase with `deliverables===undefined` and a claimed-but-absent file → `deliverableCheck.pass===true`. Evidence: `implementation.ts:185` calls `runDeliverableCheck(setup.worktreePath, phase.deliverables, …)`; `build-runner.ts:1789` runDeliverableCheck early-returns `{pass:true}` when `deliverables` is undefined — exactly the stockfan Phase-5/6 shape (no deliverables declared, claim a file, never create it).

H3 (10%, CONFIRMED, contributing condition): The build gate cannot detect a hallucinated file. Prediction: `gate.pass===true` even though file X was never created. Evidence: `runBuildGate` compiles/whatever currently exists; a file the implementer CLAIMS to create but never references compiles fine (no missing-symbol error), so build-green is orthogonal to the claim. Note: H1/H2/H3 are not mutually exclusive — their CONJUNCTION is the root cause; H1 is the dominant missing piece this fix adds (the git ground-truth), H2 is the spec-10 layer it must not regress, H3 explains why build-green alone is insufficient.
data.rootCause: Phase GREEN in `src/stages/implementation.ts:194` — `(gate.pass || gate.inScopePass) && deliverableCheck.pass` — has NO term binding the AGENT's self-reported file changes (`filesModified`, parsed `:159-160`) to git reality, and the only existing assertion (`runDeliverableCheck`) is opt-in by the spec author (undefined `deliverables` → trivial pass at `build-runner.ts:1789`). There is no per-phase git snapshot or delta, so three conditions collapse into one false-green: (1) the implementer claims `filesModified:[X]` but changes nothing in git; (2) the build passes because a non-existent/unreferenced file compiles clean; (3) deliverables pass trivially because none were declared. The claimed set is accumulated (`:92/:159-160/:219`) and echoed into the summary (`prompts.ts:127`) as an unverifiable string — pure advisory telemetry. Result: a phase can claim a deliverable it never produced and still be GREEN (the stockfan Phase-5/6 failure mode and the regression in AC-08). RECOMMENDED FIX (the spec, summarized): add `src/tracking.ts` `ChangeTracker` (begin/end at stage+phase granularity, git `rev-parse HEAD`+`status --porcelain` baseline then `diff --name-status <beginHead>` UNION `status --porcelain` delta, classify A/+untracked/M/D, never-throws → `<specDir>/change-tracker.jsonl`, reusing `spawnSync`+`dedupePreservingOrder` from `build-runner.ts:touchedFilePaths`); bracket ALL stages via an `ctx.events` "stage"/"phase" subscription (`nodes.ts:98/126/127` already emit them — minimal engine touch) and EACH phase in `implementation.ts` (begin before the attempt loop `:~140`, end after with the structured claim); switch `buildImplementPrompt` (`prompts.ts:120`) + review/fix prompt (`:140`) to structured `{filesCreated,filesModified,filesDeleted}` (legacy flat array tolerated); AND a `changeGate` into phase-green at `:194` where `claimedNotChanged` (claimed create/modify that git does NOT show) HARD-FAILS and is fed into the next attempt under `## Claimed changes not present in git — actually create/wire these` via the existing `attemptErrors`/`missingDeliverables` retry channel (respect `MAX_ATTEMPTS=:16`), `changedNotClaimed` is advisory-only, and git-unavailable → `changeGate.pass=true` (never block on infra); union `claimed.filesCreated` into deliverable `requireFiles` before `runDeliverableCheck` (`:185`) so spec-10 and tracking reinforce rather than diverge; thread the tracker via the `activeRun`/`setActiveRun`/`getActiveRun` module-singleton pattern (`extension.ts:68/131/148`) set from `state.setup` (specDirectory+worktreePath) in execute()'s entry and cleared in its finally (`extension.ts:391/446`). Theme methods stay method-style (no destructuring) to keep `tests/stream-theme-class-theme.test.ts` + `tests/render/real-theme-parity.test.ts` green.
reproductionSteps: 1. Confirm the buggy 'before' state on this worktree: `git status --short` shows only `docs/specifications/11-…/` untracked; `ls src/tracking.ts` → 'No such file'; `find . -name change-tracker.jsonl` → empty. (Verified during exploration.) The implementation has NOT started; main is the vulnerable baseline.

2. Construct a minimal reproduction WITHOUT the fix: a spec whose implementation phase declares NO `deliverables` (or deliverables that happen to pass) and an implementer agent stub returning `control:{filesModified:["src/tracking.ts"], testsPassCount:1, summary:"created tracking"}` but NOT creating that file.

3. Invoke the implementation stage's phase loop (e.g. a vitest test mirroring `tests/implementation.test.ts` / `tests/build-runner-deliverable-check.test.ts` that drives `runImplementation` / the phase for-loop directly, or a tiny synthetic pipeline via the `task()` node in `src/nodes.ts`).

4. Observe the three collapsing conditions: (a) `runBuildGate(setup.worktreePath,…)` → `gate.pass===true` (file never referenced → compiles clean); (b) `runDeliverableCheck(setup.worktreePath, undefined,…)` → early-returns `{pass:true,missing:[]}` (SCENARIO-014); (c) `implementation.ts:194` `(gate.pass||gate.inScopePass) && deliverableCheck.pass` → `true` → `green=true`.

5. Confirm the advisory lie: `implementation.ts:159-160` accumulates `filesModified:["src/tracking.ts"]` into the summary; `prompts.ts:127` echoes 'Files Modified: src/tracking.ts'. Meanwhile `git -C <worktree> status --porcelain` shows NO change to that path and the file is absent. Phase is GREEN on a vacuous claim — FALSE GREEN reproduced.

6. The FIX gates this (regression test = AC-08): after the change-tracker is added, the same phase returns structured `claimed:{filesCreated:["src/tracking.ts"]}`; the phase-end `end("phase", phaseId, claimed)` computes `git diff --name-status <beginHead>` UNION `git status --porcelain`, finds NO `A`/`??` entry for that path → `crossCheck.claimedNotChanged=["src/tracking.ts"]` non-empty → `changeGate.pass===false` → phase-green becomes `… && changeGate.pass` = false → NOT green, and `claimedNotChanged` is fed into the next attempt under `## Claimed changes not present in git — actually create/wire these`. The false-green becomes impossible; the stockfan Phase-5/6 failure class is closed a second way.

7. Verify non-regression after the fix: `npm run typecheck` clean; `npm test` all green (existing ~1120 + new tracker/cross-check/structured-changes tests); `tests/stream-theme-class-theme.test.ts` and `tests/render/real-theme-parity.test.ts` stay green (Theme called method-style, never destructured).

data.title: Codebase Assessment — Git Change-Tracker & Cross-Check Gate (spec-11)
data.summary: pi-super-dev is a self-contained TypeScript pi-extension/CLI pipeline (ESM, "type":"module", node ≥22.19, zero runtime deps — only peerDeps pi-coding-agent/typebox/pi-tui). The change-tracker fits cleanly into a mature, heavily-patterned codebase: a control-flow node algebra (src/nodes.ts) drives an EventEmitter-powered engine (src/workflow.ts) that emits "stage"/"phase" events; a deterministic oracle layer (src/build-runner.ts) already does robust, never-throwing git/spawn work with discrete-argv spawnSync and the exact committed-diff UNION untracked-files pattern the tracker needs; phase-green is already an AND-chain `(gate.pass||gate.inScopePass) && deliverableCheck.pass` with retry-prompt injection; and there is a canonical module-level per-run singleton pattern (activeRun in src/extension.ts) set in execute() entry / cleared in finally — the exact mechanism to thread a per-run ChangeTracker. There is NO HTTP API server or UI dev server in this repo; local verification is `npm run typecheck` (tsc --noEmit) and `npm test` (vitest run). The implementer's output contract is a single line in prompts.ts that returns `filesModified (array)` — advisory only — so the structured-set + cross-check gate is a small, surgical addition, not a rewrite.

patterns.name: Never-throw safe degradation on every git/spawn op
patterns.example: src/build-runner.ts:538-573 (touchedFilePaths: try { spawnSync git diff + ls-files } catch { return [] }) — returns [] on non-git dir, ENOENT, non-string stdout, or any throw
patterns.consistency: Universal in build-runner.ts (runBuildGate, runRedCheck, runDeliverableCheck all degrade to []/unknown/empty rather than throwing). The ChangeTracker MUST follow this: git unavailable → record {gitUnavailable:true} and continue, never block, never throw.

patterns.name: Discrete-argv spawnSync + timeout envelope, no shell
patterns.example: src/build-runner.ts:538-545 (spawnSync("git", ["-C", cwd, "diff", ...], { encoding:"utf8" }) — never shell:true; timeouts via resolveTimeoutMs)
patterns.consistency: Every git subprocess in the module. The tracker's rev-parse / status --porcelain / diff --name-status <beginHead> spawns must use the identical discrete-argv form to keep path data out of any shell and to inherit the timeout discipline.

patterns.name: Git delta = committed-diff UNION untracked, deduped first-seen
patterns.example: src/build-runner.ts:538-573 (git diff --merge-base <ref> --name-only UNION git ls-files --others --exclude-standard, then dedupePreservingOrder); dedupePreservingOrder defined at src/build-runner.ts:118
patterns.consistency: This is the exact union logic the tracker's gitActual classification needs — but keyed off a stored beginHead (git diff --name-status <beginHead>) rather than a base ref, plus git status --porcelain for uncommitted. Reuse dedupePreservingOrder verbatim.

patterns.name: Phase-green AND-chain with retry-prompt injection (spec-10 pattern to mirror)
patterns.example: src/stages/implementation.ts:194 (if ((gate.pass||gate.inScopePass) && deliverableCheck.pass) {...GREEN...}); failures injected at src/stages/implementation.ts:146-148 via implParts.push(`## Previous attempt failed...`) / `## Deliverables still missing — create/wire these`
patterns.consistency: The changeGate MUST slot into this exact pattern: extend line 194 to `&& changeGate.pass`, and feed claimedNotChanged into implParts under a `## Claimed changes not present in git — actually create/wire these` block mirroring the deliverable-miss block at 148, bounded by MAX_ATTEMPTS (=3, src/stages/implementation.ts:16).

patterns.name: Per-run module singleton, set in execute() entry / cleared in finally
patterns.example: src/extension.ts:68 (let activeRun) / :141 (setActiveRun on execute() entry) / :149 (getActiveRun) / :391 (null in finally) — the mid-run-input feature's threading model
patterns.consistency: The canonical mechanism for threading per-run state across stage boundaries without editing nodes.ts/workflow.ts/pipeline.ts internals. The ChangeTracker should mirror this exactly: module-level `let activeTracker`, setActiveTracker(specDir, worktreePath) in execute() entry, null in the same finally. state.setup.worktreePath + state.setup.specDirectory (src/types.ts:112-113, 292-293) supply both required paths.

patterns.name: Event-bus stage/phase seam (minimal engine touch)
patterns.example: src/nodes.ts:126-127 (ctx.events.emit("phase", stage.label) + emit("stage", {id,label,status:"running"})) and :98 (emit "stage" with status after run); subscribed at src/workflow.ts:163-164 (ctx.events.on("stage"/"phase"))
patterns.consistency: PREFERRED over editing nodes.ts internals: subscribe the tracker to ctx.events for stage enter/exit boundaries, OR add a thin tracker.begin/end wrapper around stage.run at nodes.ts:127-129. Phases still bracket inside implementation.ts since phase events are coarse (only the stage label, per-node), not per-phase-id.

patterns.name: Output contract is a single templated prompt line (easy to upgrade)
patterns.example: src/prompts.ts:120 (buildImplementPrompt ends: "Output <control> JSON with: filesModified (array), testsPassCount (number), summary.") and :140 (fix prompt: "...with: filesModified (array), fixesApplied (number), summary.")
patterns.consistency: The structured-set change is a one-line edit in two prompt builders (buildImplementPrompt + the review/fix prompt), changing filesModified (array) → filesCreated (array), filesModified (array), filesDeleted (array). No other contract plumbing.

patterns.name: Hermetic vitest tests, one file per concern, fully mocked git/agent
patterns.example: tests/implementation-deliverable-wiring.test.ts:24-40 (vi.mock("../src/build-runner.ts"), stub runRedCheck to "unknown", mock renderAndWrite — disk-free); naming: build-runner-<feature>.test.ts / implementation-<feature>-wiring.test.ts
patterns.consistency: 1120 existing tests all follow this. New tracker tests must vi.mock spawnSync (or the tracker's git helper), assert change-tracker.jsonl content + append-only behavior, and follow build-runner-tracker.test.ts / implementation-crosscheck-gate.test.ts naming. .ts extensions in all imports; ESM import paths.

recommendations: Mirror the activeRun singleton (src/extension.ts:68/141/149/391) for the ChangeTracker: a module-level `let activeTracker` in a new src/tracking.ts, set setActiveTracker(specDir, worktreePath) on execute() entry right after setActiveRun, nulled in the SAME finally. This threads both required paths (from state.setup, src/types.ts:112-113) with zero edits to nodes.ts/workflow.ts/pipeline.ts internals.

Reuse build-runner.ts's git primitives, do not re-implement: import dedupePreservingOrder and copy the two-spawn discrete-argv union shape (src/build-runner.ts:538-573). For the tracker's delta use `git diff --name-status <beginHead>` (status letters A/M/D map directly to created/modified/deleted) UNION `git status --porcelain`, wrapped in one try/catch that records {gitUnavailable:true} on any failure and continues (never throws, never blocks — the established never-throw contract).

Slot the changeGate into the existing phase-green AND at src/stages/implementation.ts:194 — extend to `(gate.pass||gate.inScopePass) && deliverableCheck.pass && changeGate.pass`. Feed claimedNotChanged into the next implementer retry under a `## Claimed changes not present in git — actually create/wire these` block, mirroring the missingDeliverables block at line 148, bounded by MAX_ATTEMPTS. changedNotClaimed stays advisory-only (ctx.log), and git-unavailable → changeGate.pass = true (don't block on infra).

Bracket phases inside implementation.ts around the `for (const [idx, phase] of phases.entries())` loop (src/stages/implementation.ts ~93): tracker.begin("phase", phaseId) before the attempt loop, tracker.end("phase", phaseId, claimedFromImplementer) after. Bracket ALL stages via a ctx.events subscription in src/workflow.ts:163-164 (stage enter/exit) — the minimal-touch seam — rather than editing nodes.ts stage.run internals. Emit begin events as {event:"start"} and end as {event:"end"} into <specDir>/change-tracker.jsonl so the bracket trace is complete.

Bridge to spec-10 by unioning claimed.filesCreated into the deliverable requireFiles before calling runDeliverableCheck (src/build-runner.ts:1830, DeliverableContract.requireFiles at :1588) — a claimed-created file must exist, so tracking and deliverable-assertions reinforce. Update the prompt contract line at src/prompts.ts:120 and :140 from `filesModified (array)` to the structured `filesCreated/filesModified/filesDeleted` set, with a one-line 'these are git-cross-checked; claiming a file you didn't change fails the phase' nudge. Accept legacy flat filesModified arrays defensively (tolerate both control shapes) — same defensive cast already used at src/stages/implementation.ts:159.

data.title: Git Change-Tracker with Per-Stage/Per-Phase Bracketing and a Claimed-vs-Actual Cross-Check Gate (spec-11)
data.summary: Replace the advisory flat `filesModified: string[]` returned by code-mutating agents with a structured `{filesCreated, filesModified, filesDeleted}` set AND add a new `src/tracking.ts` `ChangeTracker` that brackets EVERY stage (start+end) and EVERY implementation phase (start+end) with a git snapshot, persisting an append-only `<specDir>/change-tracker.jsonl` of begin/end records. Each end record carries a git-derived `gitActual` change set, the agent's claimed set, and a one-directional cross-check (`claimedNotChanged` vs `changedNotClaimed`). A new `changeGate` is AND-ed into phase-green alongside the existing build gate and spec-10 deliverable check, so a phase that CLAIMS to create/modify a file git does NOT show changed hard-fails (the false-green root cause closed a second way), while `changedNotClaimed` (under-reporting) stays advisory. The whole subsystem NEVER throws and degrades when git is unavailable (`gitUnavailable` → record + `changeGate.pass = true`, never block). Tracking and spec-10 deliverable assertions collapse into one enforcement path: `claimed.filesCreated` auto-unions into `deliverables.requireFiles`, and the flat `filesModified` accumulation is derived from the structured set so existing summary/dashboard writers keep working. Pure TypeScript change to this repo (pi-super-dev), zero new runtime deps, discrete-argv `spawnSync` git ops under the existing timeout envelope. Verification: `npm run typecheck` clean and `npm test` all green (existing 1120 + new), with no regression to runRedCheck, runDeliverableCheck, npm in-scope classification, scope-aware cargo gate, themed stream, mid-run input injection, dashboard, or real-theme parity. Covers AC-01..AC-11 and SCENARIO-001..SCENARIO-020.
data.architecture:
## System context

pi-super-dev is a self-contained TypeScript (ESM, zero-runtime-deps) pi-extension/CLI driving a 13-stage pipeline. The pipeline is a tree of control-flow nodes (src/nodes.ts) executed by an EventEmitter-powered engine (src/workflow.ts); stages run via the `task()` leaf node which emits `ctx.events` "stage"/"phase" events around `stage.run`. A deterministic oracle layer (src/build-runner.ts) already performs robust, never-throwing git/spawn work using discrete-argv `spawnSync` and a committed-diff UNION untracked-files pattern (`touchedFilePaths`, `dedupePreservingOrder`). Phase-green is already an AND-chain in src/stages/implementation.ts: `(gate.pass || gate.inScopePass) && deliverableCheck.pass`, with retry-prompt injection (`attemptErrors`, `missingDeliverables` → `## ... still missing — create/wire these`) bounded by `MAX_ATTEMPTS = 3`. A canonical per-run module singleton (`activeRun`/`setActiveRun`/`getActiveRun` in src/extension.ts) is set on `execute()` entry and nulled in the `finally`. This spec slots the change-tracker cleanly into those existing patterns with minimal engine touch.

## Core component: `src/tracking.ts` (NEW — AC-01, AC-02, AC-03)

A per-run `ChangeTracker` class constructed with `(specDir: string, worktreePath: string)`. It owns a `Map<unitKey, Baseline>` of pending begin-baselines and writes a single durable append-only file `<specDir>/change-tracker.jsonl` (one record per begin/end event, never overwritten).

**Public API (contract-first signatures):**
```ts
type TrackerUnit = "stage" | "phase";
interface StructuredChanges { filesCreated: string[]; filesModified: string[]; filesDeleted: string[]; }
interface GitActual { created: string[]; modified: string[]; deleted: string[]; }
interface CrossCheck { claimedNotChanged: string[]; changedNotClaimed: string[]; }
interface ChangeRecord {
  unit: TrackerUnit; id: string; event: "start" | "end"; ts: string;
  beginHead: string | null; endHead: string | null;
  gitActual: GitActual | null;
  claimed: StructuredChanges | null;
  crossCheck: CrossCheck | null;
  verdict: "ok" | "claimed-miss" | "git-unavailable";
  gitUnavailable?: boolean;
}
class ChangeTracker {
  constructor(specDir: string, worktreePath: string);
  begin(unit: TrackerUnit, id: string): void;
  end(unit: TrackerUnit, id: string, claimed?: StructuredChanges): ChangeRecord | null;
  getRecord(unit: TrackerUnit, id: string): ChangeRecord | null;  // last end-record (gate reads crossCheck)
}
```

**`begin(unit, id)`**: snapshot baseline = `git -C <wt> rev-parse HEAD` (committed ref) UNION `git -C <wt> status --porcelain` (working-tree state); stash both keyed by `${unit}:${id}`. Emits one `{event:"start", unit, id, ts, beginHead}` jsonl line (complete bracket trace — recommended per the open question).

**`end(unit, id, claimed?)`**: re-snapshot; compute the DELTA as `git -C <wt> diff --name-status <beginHead>` (committed changes since begin, status letters `A`/`M`/`D` map directly to created/modified/deleted) UNION `git -C <wt> status --porcelain` (uncommitted/untracked — `??`→created, `M`/`MM`→modified, `D`/`DM`→deleted). Normalize porcelain XY-codes via an explicit `classifyPorcelain(xy: string)` mapping (no ambiguity: `??`=created, `D*`/`*D`=deleted, else modified). Union both with `dedupePreservingOrder` (exported from build-runner.ts — see §Reuse). Build `gitActual`, then cross-check: `claimedNotChanged = (claimed.filesCreated ∪ claimed.filesModified) \ gitActual.{created∪modified}`, `changedNotClaimed = gitActual.{created∪modified∪deleted} \ claimed.{all three}` (advisory). Append ONE `{event:"end", ...}` jsonl line. Return the `ChangeRecord`.

**Never-throw invariant (AC-02):** the entire `begin`/`end` body and every git op is wrapped in one `try/catch`. On any git failure (ENOENT, non-zero exit, non-string stdout, spawn error, non-git dir) the record carries `{gitUnavailable: true, gitActual: null, crossCheck: null, verdict: "git-unavailable"}` and the method returns that record (never throws, never blocks). Conservative parse (SCENARIO-006): a claimed file is recorded as `claimedNotChanged` ONLY when `gitActual` was successfully computed and the file is absent from `gitActual.created∪modified` — if git was unavailable or the parse ambiguous, `claimedNotChanged` stays empty (no false failure).

## Git primitives & reuse (AC-01 — no new runtime deps)

- **Export `dedupePreservingOrder`** from `src/build-runner.ts` (currently module-private at ~line 112) and import it into `tracking.ts`. Single source of truth, no duplication.
- The tracker's three git spawns (`rev-parse HEAD`, `status --porcelain`, `diff --name-status <beginHead>`) copy the EXACT discrete-argv `spawnSync` shape from `touchedFilePaths` (build-runner.ts ~538): `spawnSync("git", ["-C", worktreePath, ...], { encoding: "utf8", timeout: resolveTimeoutMs(...) })`. Discrete argv (never `shell:true`) keeps agent-supplied file paths out of any shell (security/hygiene). A `gitSpawn(argv)` private helper centralizes the try/catch + the timeout envelope.
- The committed-diff UNION untracked-files pattern is `touchedFilePaths`'s exact approach, but keyed off the stored `beginHead` instead of a base ref.

## Threading: per-run singleton mirroring `activeRun` (AC-05)

Add `let activeTracker: ChangeTracker | null` + `setActiveTracker(t | null)` + `getActiveTracker(): ChangeTracker | null` to `src/tracking.ts` (exported; mirrors extension.ts:68/141/149). **Key design decision:** `state.setup` (which supplies `worktreePath` + `specDirectory`, src/types.ts:112-113) is NOT available at `execute()` entry — it is established inside the pipeline run. Therefore `setActiveTracker(new ChangeTracker(specDir, worktreePath))` is called in `src/pipeline.ts` (runPipelineTask) at the point `state.setup` is finalized (right after the setup stage populates the worktree + spec dir, before the producing stages run), guarded so a stale singleton from an overlapping run is discarded first (same guard activeRun uses). It is nulled in the same `finally` that nulls `activeRun` in `extension.ts` execute() (add `setActiveTracker(null)` adjacent to `setActiveRun(null)`). No leak between runs (SCENARIO-010). Stages/phases read it via `getActiveTracker()` and no-op when null (idle / non-git) — never throw.

## Bracketing EVERY stage and EVERY phase (AC-04)

**Stages — minimal-touch event seam (PREFERRED):** `task()` in `src/nodes.ts` already emits `ctx.events.emit("stage", { id, label, status: "running" })` immediately before `stage.run` and `record(ctx, <terminal status>)` → `ctx.events.emit("stage", {id,label,status})` after. Subscribe the tracker to the "stage" channel in `src/workflow.ts` (where `ctx.events.on("stage"/"phase")` is ALREADY wired per code-assessment): on `status === "running"` → `tracker.begin("stage", id)`; on terminal status (`ok`/`failed`/`skipped`) → `tracker.end("stage", id)` (stages have no structured claim → `claimed = undefined`). This is the surgical seam: NO edit to nodes.ts/workflow.ts internals, only an added subscription block + the begin/end calls. (Fallback only if events lack stage id: a thin wrapper around `stage.run` in nodes.ts:127-129 — explicitly dis-preferred, documented as the contingency.)

**Implementation phases — bracket inside `src/stages/implementation.ts`:** wrap each phase iteration `for (const [idx, phase] of phases.entries())` with `tracker.begin("phase", phaseId)` (before the attempt loop) and `tracker.end("phase", phaseId, claimedFromImplementer)` (after the loop, using the structured changes parsed from the last implementer control). Produces the required nesting: `stage-start → phase1-start → phase1-end → … → stage-end` (SCENARIO-009). `phaseId` already exists (`phase-${pad(idx+1)}`). Tracker calls are guarded by `const tracker = getActiveTracker(); if (tracker) { ... }`.

## Structured change set (AC-06, AC-10)

- **prompts.ts:** `buildImplementPrompt` (~line 120) and `buildFixPrompt` (~line 140) change their trailing contract line from `filesModified (array)` to `filesCreated (array), filesModified (array), filesDeleted (array)` with the one-line instruction: *"Report the exact files you created/modified/deleted — these are git-cross-checked; claiming a file you did not change fails the phase."* `buildImplementationSummaryPrompt` (~line 127) is unchanged but now consumes the derived flat list (AC-10).
- **implementation.ts:** add `parseStructuredChanges(control): StructuredChanges` that reads `filesCreated/filesModified/filesDeleted` when present, and backward-tolerates a legacy flat `filesModified` array (normalize into `filesModified`, leaving created/deleted empty). The per-attempt accumulation (implementation.ts ~line 92/159/219) derives the flat `filesModified[]` shown in the summary by unioning `filesCreated ∪ filesModified` (deleted excluded from the "modified" display) — so the existing summary writer keeps working byte-identically for unchanged agents (AC-10, SCENARIO-019). Add a `📝 N files changed (C/M/D)` evidence line to the summary, e.g. `📝 3 files changed (1C/2M/0D)` from the phase end-record's `gitActual`.

## Git cross-check GATE (AC-07, AC-08) — the false-green killer

In `src/stages/implementation.ts`, after `runBuildGate` (~165) and `runDeliverableCheck` (~185), compute:
```ts
const changeRec = tracker?.getRecord("phase", phaseId) ?? null;
const changeGate = computeChangeGate(changeRec);   // never throws
```
where `computeChangeGate(rec)` returns `{pass: boolean, claimedNotChanged: string[]}`:
- `pass === false` iff `rec != null && !rec.gitUnavailable && (rec.crossCheck?.claimedNotChanged?.length ?? 0) > 0` — a created/modified claim git does NOT show (the false-green killer, AC-08).
- `changedNotClaimed` is advisory-only: logged via `ctx.log(...)`, never affects `pass` (SCENARIO-014 — under-reporting is not a false-green).
- `gitUnavailable` (or no tracker) → `pass = true` (don't block on infrastructure, SCENARIO-017). Never throws.
- No claimed changes → `claimedNotChanged` empty → `pass = true` (SCENARIO-016, trivial pass).

Phase GREEN (implementation.ts ~line 194) becomes:
```ts
if ((gate.pass || gate.inScopePass) && deliverableCheck.pass && changeGate.pass) { green = true; ... break; }
```
On a `changeGate` miss, feed `claimedNotChanged` into the next implementer attempt via the EXISTING retry channel — a new `## Claimed changes not present in git — actually create/wire these` block pushed onto `implParts` (mirroring the `missingDeliverables` block at ~line 148), bounded by `MAX_ATTEMPTS`. So a claimed-but-never-created file (`claimed:{filesCreated:[X]}` with no new file in git) → `changeGate.pass === false` → phase NOT green even when build + deliverable both pass → the stockfan Phase-5/6 false-green becomes impossible (SCENARIO-013).

## Spec-10 deliverable bridge (AC-09)

Before calling `runDeliverableCheck`, UNION `claimed.filesCreated` with any spec-declared `phase.deliverables.requireFiles` (build-runner.ts `DeliverableContract.requireFiles` ~line 1588) into the contract actually passed to `runDeliverableCheck`. So a file a phase claims to have created MUST exist — tracking and deliverable assertions reinforce. No circular double-count: if an agent omits a spec-required file, the spec-declared `requireFiles` still catches it independently (confirmed in the requirements open question).

## Surfacing (AC-10)

The phase end-record's `gitActual` is the ground-truth evidence: the `📝 N files changed (C/M/D)` line in the implementation summary, and the flat `filesModified` accumulation is derived from the structured set. The dashboard continues to read the existing summary shape (no dashboard change required this cut; a dedicated change-tracker panel consuming `change-tracker.jsonl` is listed as future surfacing).

## Constraints honored
- NEVER destructure pi `Theme` methods (class with `this.fgColors`); call method-style — `tests/stream-theme-class-theme.test.ts` + `tests/render/real-theme-parity.test.ts` guard this and must stay green.
- NEVER throw from the tracker, git ops, cross-check, or gate — always degrade.
- Backward compatible: legacy flat `filesModified` arrays accepted; non-git worktrees recorded `gitUnavailable` and unblock; phases without claims trivially pass.
- No new runtime deps; git spawns reuse `spawnSync` + `resolveTimeoutMs`; nothing cached across a phase.
- Minimal engine touch: stage bracketing via an event subscription; phase bracketing inside implementation.ts; singleton set/clear in pipeline.ts + the existing finally.
data.testingStrategy:
The verification strategy is layered to match the three granularities of the feature (module → wiring → gate), with every test hermetic (fully-mocked git/agent, disk-free where possible), following the repo's vitest conventions (`vi.mock("../src/build-runner.ts")`, stub runRedCheck/runDeliverableCheck, mock renderAndWrite; `.ts` import extensions; one file per concern; naming `build-runner-<feature>.test.ts` / `implementation-<feature>-wiring.test.ts`). Local gate: `npm run typecheck` (tsc --noEmit) clean + `npm test` (vitest run) ALL green. The repo has no Rust, so there is no cargo gate (gate field reflects this).

**Layer 1 — ChangeTracker unit tests (`tests/tracking.test.ts`, AC-01/AC-02/AC-03 → SCENARIO-001..007):** `vi.mock("node:child_process", ...)` to return scripted git outputs. Assert: (a) begin captures a baseline and holds it pending; (b) created/modified/deleted classification from `diff --name-status` (A→created, M→modified, D→deleted) UNION `status --porcelain` (`??`→created, `D`→deleted, `M`→modified); (c) `claimedNotChanged` (claim file `a.ts` created; git shows no `a.ts`) vs `changedNotClaimed` (git shows `b.ts` modified; agent didn't report) split correctly; (d) git-unavailable (mock throws / non-zero exit / non-string stdout) → record `{gitUnavailable:true}`, `verdict:"git-unavailable"`, method returns without throwing, no block; (e) conservative parse — ambiguous/unavailable parse leaves `claimedNotChanged` empty (no false failure, SCENARIO-006); (f) begin/end bracketing emits BOTH a `start` and an `end` jsonl line; (g) append-only — multiple `end` events produce multiple lines, nothing overwritten; (h) `dedupePreservingOrder` reuse collapses a path present in both committed-diff and porcelain to one entry at first-seen position.

**Layer 2 — Parsing + prompt-contract + singleton tests (`tests/structured-changes.test.ts`, AC-05/AC-06 → SCENARIO-010..012):** (a) `parseStructuredChanges` reads `{filesCreated,filesModified,filesDeleted}` and normalizes a legacy flat `filesModified` array (created/deleted empty) without error; (b) snapshot assertions that `buildImplementPrompt` and `buildFixPrompt` end with the structured-set contract line and the cross-check warning text; (c) the flat summary `filesModified[]` derivation unions created+modified and excludes deleted; (d) singleton lifecycle — `setActiveTracker(t)` then `getActiveTracker()===t`; after `setActiveTracker(null)` (the finally path) a second call returns null (no leak, SCENARIO-010), and a stale singleton is discarded when a fresh one is set mid-run.

**Layer 3 — Bracketing wiring test (`tests/tracker-bracketing.test.ts`, AC-04 → SCENARIO-008/009):** run a tiny synthetic pipeline of ≥2 task() stages plus the implementation stage (mocked agents that touch a temp git worktree via the real spawnSync, or assert on emitted jsonl via a tracker pointed at a temp specDir). Assert `change-tracker.jsonl` contains `stage-start`+`stage-end` for EVERY stage AND `phase-start`+`phase-end` for the implementation stage, in correct nested order (`stage-start → phase1-start → phase1-end → phase2-start → phase2-end → stage-end`). This validates the event subscription seam and the implementation.ts bracket placement without depending on a full real run.

**Layer 4 — changeGate regression tests (`tests/implementation-crosscheck-gate.test.ts`, AC-07/AC-08 → SCENARIO-013..017):** mirror `tests/implementation.test.ts` / `build-runner-deliverable-check.test.ts`. `vi.mock` the build-runner (stub `runRedCheck`→"unknown", `runBuildGate`→{pass:true}, `runDeliverableCheck`→{pass:true}) so the ONLY variable is the changeGate. Scenarios: (a) **false-green killer** — phase returns `claimed:{filesCreated:["x.ts"]}` but git shows NO new file → `changeGate.pass === false` → phase NOT green even though build+deliverable pass (SCENARIO-013, AC-08); (b) `changedNotClaimed` only (agent under-reported, git shows extra edits) → `changeGate.pass === true`, advisory logged, phase green (SCENARIO-014); (c) `claimedNotChanged` fed into the next implementer attempt under `## Claimed changes not present in git — actually create/wire these`, respecting `MAX_ATTEMPTS`, and a fix on retry → green (SCENARIO-015); (d) no claimed changes → trivial pass (SCENARIO-016); (e) `gitUnavailable` → `changeGate.pass === true`, no throw, no block (SCENARIO-017).

**Layer 5 — Spec-10 bridge test (`tests/implementation-deliverable-bridge.test.ts` or extend the existing deliverable-wiring test, AC-09 → SCENARIO-018):** assert `claimed.filesCreated` is UNIONed into `requireFiles` before `runDeliverableCheck` is invoked (a created file that does not exist → deliverable miss), and that spec-declared `requireFiles` still independently catch an omitted file (no circular double-count). Add the `📝 N files changed (C/M/D)` summary-line assertion here too (AC-10 → SCENARIO-019).

**Layer 6 — Non-regression (AC-11 → SCENARIO-020):** `npm run typecheck` clean; `npm test` all green with the existing 1120 tests untouched plus the new files above. Specifically confirm no regression to: runRedCheck wiring, runDeliverableCheck + cache reset, npm in-scope classification, scope-aware cargo gate, themed live stream, mid-run input injection (the `activeRun` singleton coexists with `activeTracker`), the dashboard widget, and real-theme parity (`tests/stream-theme-class-theme.test.ts`, `tests/render/real-theme-parity.test.ts` stay green — pi `Theme` called method-style, never destructured). A grep audit that no code added a destructured `Theme` method call closes the theme-parity constraint.

**Coverage matrix:** SCENARIO-001/002/003/004 → Layer 1 (AC-01); SCENARIO-005/006 → Layer 1 git-unavailable + conservative parse (AC-02); SCENARIO-007 → Layer 1 bracketing + append-only (AC-03); SCENARIO-008/009 → Layer 3 (AC-04); SCENARIO-010 → Layer 2 singleton (AC-05); SCENARIO-011/012 → Layer 2 prompts/parse (AC-06); SCENARIO-013 → Layer 4(a) (AC-08); SCENARIO-014/015/016/017 → Layer 4 (AC-07); SCENARIO-018 → Layer 5 (AC-09); SCENARIO-019 → Layer 5 summary (AC-10); SCENARIO-020 → Layer 6 (AC-11). All 20 scenarios and 11 ACs covered; uncovered = 0.

phases.name: Phase 1 — ChangeTracker core module (src/tracking.ts)
phases.description: DOMAIN: tracking. AC-01, AC-02, AC-03 → SCENARIO-001..007. Create the self-contained ChangeTracker class with begin/end at stage|phase granularity, the git baseline (rev-parse HEAD + status --porcelain), the delta computation (diff --name-status <beginHead> UNION status --porcelain), the created/modified/deleted classification (A→created, M→modified, D→deleted; porcelain ??→created, D→deleted, else modified via an explicit classifyPorcelain map), the one-directional cross-check (claimedNotChanged vs changedNotClaimed), the append-only <specDir>/change-tracker.jsonl writer (start + end records), and the never-throw contract (git failure → {gitUnavailable:true}, conservative parse leaves claimedNotChanged empty on ambiguity). Export the ChangeRecord/StructuredChanges/CrossCheck/TrackerUnit types and a private gitSpawn(argv) helper reusing spawnSync + resolveTimeoutMs. Export dedupePreservingOrder from src/build-runner.ts (currently module-private ~line 112) and import it. Independently testable: pure module + unit tests with mocked git, NO engine integration, NO dependency on later phases. Unit tests (tests/tracking.test.ts) cover classification, cross-check split, git-unavailable→record-and-continue, start+end bracketing, append-only jsonl, dedup positioning.

phases.name: Phase 3 — Stage + phase bracketing wiring + summary surfacing
phases.description: DOMAIN: engine, tracking. AC-04, AC-10 → SCENARIO-008, SCENARIO-009, SCENARIO-019. DEPENDS ON Phases 1+2 (uses getActiveTracker() + the structured parse). (a) Stage bracketing via the minimal-touch event seam: in src/workflow.ts add a ctx.events 'stage' subscription — on status==='running' → getActiveTracker()?.begin('stage', id); on terminal status (ok/failed/skipped) → getActiveTracker()?.end('stage', id) (no claimed set for stages). (a) Add computeChangeGate(rec) helper (in src/tracking.ts or src/build-runner.ts — pick build-runner.ts for co-location with the other gates): returns {pass:boolean, claimedNotChanged:string[]} where pass===false iff rec && !rec.gitUnavailable && (rec.crossCheck?.claimedNotChanged?.length ?? 0) > 0; gitUnavailable/no-tracker → pass=true; never throws. data.title: Spec-11 Review — Git Change-Tracker with Per-Stage/Per-Phase Bracketing and Claimed-vs-Actual Cross-Check Gate
data.summary: Fagan-style content inspection of spec-11. The specification is exceptionally well-grounded: ~93% of file/line/API references verified against the actual codebase, with near-exact line citations in implementation.ts (phase loop @94, runBuildGate @165, runDeliverableCheck @185, phase-green @194), types.ts (state.setup @112-113), extension.ts (activeRun singleton @68/131/148, finally @442/446), nodes.ts (stage "running" emit @127 → stage.run @129, record() @98), workflow.ts (ctx.events.on stage/phase @163-164), prompts.ts (buildImplementPrompt @117/120, buildFixPrompt @135/140), and build-runner.ts (dedupePreservingOrder @118 module-private, touchedFilePaths @526, requireFiles @1588, resolveTimeoutMs @84). All 11 ACs map to spec sections; all 20 scenarios map to 6 concrete test layers in an explicit coverage matrix; zero uncovered. Architecture fits existing patterns (singleton mirror of activeRun, event-subscription seam for stage bracketing, AND-chain into phase-green, never-throw invariant). No critical defects. Two MEDIUM findings block a clean APPROVED: (1) the "EXACT spawnSync shape from touchedFilePaths" claim is inaccurate — touchedFilePaths' spawns omit the timeout the spec says it copies; (2) the setActiveTracker insertion point inside runPipelineTask is unspecified and the state.setup population locus could not be located in src, leaving the most complex wiring task under-specified. Three additional LOW findings address end-without-begin semantics, git-command divergence from touchedFilePaths, and unbounded jsonl growth. Grounding score ~93% (>90% threshold). Verdict: APPROVED WITH REVISIONS — address the two MEDIUM items before implementation to avoid a wrong insertion point and a misleading canonical-pattern citation.

findings.title: D6 Grounding — touchedFilePaths spawn omits timeout; "EXACT shape" citation is inaccurate
findings.detail: Spec §"Git primitives & reuse" states the tracker copies "the EXACT discrete-argv spawnSync shape from touchedFilePaths (build-runner.ts ~538): spawnSync(..., { encoding: 'utf8', timeout: resolveTimeoutMs(...) })". Verified against source: touchedFilePaths' two spawns (build-runner.ts:538 `diff --merge-base` and :541 `ls-files --others`) pass ONLY `{ encoding: "utf8" }` — NO `timeout` field. The timeout envelope actually lives in the OTHER spawnSync call sites (build-runner.ts:1283, 1533, 1768, all using `timeout: timeoutMs` from `resolveTimeoutMs`). So the citation conflates two different shapes. Functional impact is low (adding a timeout to the tracker's spawns is safe and desirable), but the grounding claim is wrong and could mislead the implementer about which call site is the canonical pattern. Recommendation: cite the timeout-bearing spawnSync shape generically (the 1283/1533/1768 family) rather than touchedFilePaths, OR explicitly note that touchedFilePaths is being AUGMENTED with a timeout for the tracker. Lens: D6 Grounding.

findings.title: D3/D8 — setActiveTracker insertion point in runPipelineTask is unspecified; state.setup locus unverified
findings.detail: Spec §"Threading" (AC-05) instructs: "setActiveTracker(new ChangeTracker(specDir, worktreePath)) is called in src/pipeline.ts (runPipelineTask) at the point state.setup is finalized (right after the setup stage populates the worktree + spec dir, before the producing stages run)". runPipelineTask exists at pipeline.ts:16 but builds/executes a node tree; no `state.setup =` assignment is locatable anywhere in src/ via search, and there is no named post-setup hook or line in runPipelineTask. Because setup runs as a node inside the tree, there is no clean synchronous "right after setup" insertion line in runPipelineTask itself — wiring there risks either reading setup before it is populated (undefined worktreePath/specDirectory) or missing the producing stages' brackets. This is the single most under-specified wiring point in the spec and directly affects Phase 2's testability (SCENARIO-010 singleton lifecycle assumes correct placement). Recommendation: name the concrete mechanism — e.g. subscribe to a terminal "setup" stage event, inject inside the setup stage node's return path, or add an explicit post-setup callback — and state whether the setup stage itself is intentionally excluded from bracketing (it will be, since the tracker is not yet active when setup's stage-start event fires). Lens: D3 Feasibility + D8 Ambiguity.

findings.title: D1/D8 — end() called without a matching begin() (null beginHead) behavior unspecified
findings.detail: ChangeRecord allows `beginHead: string | null`, and end() computes the delta via `git diff --name-status <beginHead>`. If end() fires for a unit with no prior begin() (null beginHead) — e.g. the setup stage whose stage-start event predates setActiveTracker, or any phase/stage that began before the singleton was wired — `diff --name-status <null>` is an invalid argv. The never-throw catch would swallow this and emit gitUnavailable:true, but that conflates "no baseline captured" with "git broken", producing a misleading verdict and a conservative pass that hides a real bracketing gap. Recommendation: specify end-without-begin explicitly (e.g. short-circuit to a record with gitActual=null, crossCheck=null, verdict="ok", skip the diff spawn) so the jsonl trace distinguishes "untracked" from "git-unavailable". Matters for SCENARIO-008/009 nested-order correctness if any stage escapes bracketing. Lens: D1 Completeness + D8 Ambiguity.

findings.title: D6/D2 — tracker git command set diverges from touchedFilePaths; working-tree semantics not reconciled
findings.detail: The tracker uses `rev-parse HEAD` + `status --porcelain` + `diff --name-status <beginHead>`, while the reused touchedFilePaths (build-runner.ts:526) uses `diff --merge-base <ref> --name-only` + `ls-files --others --exclude-standard`. The spec calls this "the committed-diff UNION untracked-files pattern keyed off beginHead instead of a base ref" — a fair justification — but `status --porcelain` is strictly BROADER than `ls-files --others --exclude-standard`: porcelain also surfaces unstaged-modified AND staged-not-committed entries via XY-codes, so gitActual.created∪modified∪deleted will include working-tree state that touchedFilePaths' untracked-only union would not. This affects the crossCheck semantics (claimedNotChanged / changedNotClaimed) and the spec-10 deliverable bridge (claimed.filesCreated UNIONed into requireFiles). Recommendation: explicitly confirm `status --porcelain` is intentional (it is the right choice for per-phase bracketing since begin..end spans working-tree mutations, not just commits), state that classifyPorcelain covers the broader state intentionally, and note the deliberate divergence from touchedFilePaths' untracked-only model so the implementer does not "harmonize" them and silently narrow gitActual. Lens: D6 Grounding + D2 Consistency.

findings.title: D7/D1 — append-only change-tracker.jsonl has no growth/rotation/cleanup NFR
findings.detail: The tracker writes an append-only `<specDir>/change-tracker.jsonl` with one record per begin/end event. Across many runs/phases/attempts (MAX_ATTEMPTS=3 × phases × stages × runs) this file grows unboundedly inside specDir, and the spec lists no rotation, truncation-on-new-run, or size NFR. The spec-10/dashboard surfacing section explicitly defers a dedicated panel to "future surfacing", so today the file is write-only. Recommendation: add a one-line NFR — e.g. truncate-on-new-run (the singleton construction point, tied to F2's resolution, is a natural reset point) or a documented cap — so the file does not accumulate indefinitely and so test Layer 1/3 assertions on append-only behavior are scoped to a single run. Lens: D7 Complexity + D1 Completeness.

dimensions.name: D1 Completeness
dimensions.notes: All 11 ACs have spec sections; all 20 scenarios mapped to 6 test layers in an explicit coverage matrix (zero uncovered). Error/edge paths specified: never-throw invariant, gitUnavailable→pass, conservative parse, legacy tolerance, no-claim trivial pass, MAX_ATTEMPTS-bound retry. NFRs covered (zero new deps, backward compat, theme parity, no Rust/cargo gate). Gaps: no end-without-begin behavior (F3); no jsonl growth/rotation NFR (F5).

dimensions.name: D2 Consistency
dimensions.notes: Terminology uniform throughout (StructuredChanges, GitActual, CrossCheck, ChangeRecord, changeGate, claimedNotChanged/changedNotClaimed). API signatures contract-first and stable across sections. Line refs drift within tolerance (setActiveRun actual @131 vs spec ~141; dedupePreservingOrder @118 vs ~112). Git-command divergence from touchedFilePaths is the one consistency seam (F4).

dimensions.name: D3 Feasibility
dimensions.notes: Architecture fits existing patterns cleanly: singleton mirrors activeRun; stage bracketing via the real ctx.events.on("stage") seam (workflow.ts:164); phase bracketing inside implementation.ts; changeGate AND-ed into the existing phase-green chain (gate.pass||inScopePass && deliverableCheck.pass). Spec-10 bridge explicitly avoids circular double-count. No circular deps. ONE feasibility risk: the setActiveTracker insertion point in runPipelineTask is not concretely specified and state.setup's population locus is unverified (F2).

dimensions.name: D4 Testability
dimensions.notes: 6 hermetic test layers with concrete mocking strategy (vi.mock node:child_process for git; stub runRedCheck/runBuildGate/runDeliverableCheck; mock renderAndWrite). Numeric thresholds explicit (MAX_ATTEMPTS=3, 1120 existing tests baseline). Each scenario has an assertable outcome (changeGate.pass boolean, jsonl line presence/order, no-throw, no-leak). Local gate: npm run typecheck && npm test both exit 0.

dimensions.name: D5 Traceability
dimensions.notes: Full coverage matrix maps SCENARIO-001..020 → AC-01..011 → test layer → spec section. Phases 1-5 decompose cleanly onto ACs and dependencies are declared (Phase 2 depends on 1; 3 on 1+2; 4 on 3; 5 on 1-4). AC→spec, SCENARIO→task, plan→task-list chains all unbroken.

dimensions.name: D6 Grounding
dimensions.notes: ~93% of references verified against source (above 90% gate). implementation.ts line refs near-exact (phase loop @94, runBuildGate @165, runDeliverableCheck @185, phase-green @194, accumulation @159, decl @92, return @219). types.ts state.setup @112-113 exact. extension.ts singleton @68/131/148, finally @442/446. nodes.ts stage emit @127/run @129/record @98. workflow.ts events @163-164. prompts.ts @117/120/126/135/140. build-runner.ts dedupePreservingOrder @118/touchedFilePaths @526/requireFiles @1588/resolveTimeoutMs @84. Deductions: touchedFilePaths spawn omits timeout so "EXACT shape" claim is wrong (F1); git-command divergence undocumented (F4); state.setup locus unverified (F2).

dimensions.name: D7 Complexity
dimensions.notes: One new self-contained module + minimal engine touch (event subscription + implementation.ts bracketing + singleton set/clear). Reuses dedupePreservingOrder (single source of truth, no duplication) and the existing gate AND-chain. Simplest viable approach (no new deps, discrete-argv spawnSync under existing timeout envelope). Only complexity concern: unbounded append-only jsonl with no rotation (F5).

dimensions.name: D8 Ambiguity
dimensions.notes: API schemas fully defined (ChangeRecord, StructuredChanges, GitActual, CrossCheck, computeChangeGate return shape). State transitions explicit (begin→baseline, end→delta+crossCheck, gate→pass/claimedNotChanged). Error responses specified (gitUnavailable→record+pass, never-throw). Ambiguity gaps: setActiveTracker insertion point (F2) and end-without-begin/null-beginHead semantics (F3) are the two under-specified behaviors that could cause implementation drift.
